needed_when: repository operations require MCP instead of native platform tooling
agent must inspect branches, pull requests, commits or repository metadata through MCP
agent must read or update issues, work items, boards, milestones or sprints through MCP
task requires migrations against a real environment
task requires validating integration with real data
safety: never store database credentials in repository files
prefer read-only credentials when inspection is enough
require explicit human approval before write operations

needed_when: local project file access is required through MCP
platform does not already provide repository file access
safety: avoid exposing home directory or secrets folders

needed_when: UI validation cannot be performed through tests alone
safety: avoid logging into sensitive accounts unless explicitly approved

needed_when: task requires environment inspection through MCP
safety: never store deployment credentials in repository files
require explicit human approval for production actions

servers.name: example-filesystem
notes: Example only. Adapt to the selected platform.

servers.name: example-github
notes: Prefer the platform's native GitHub integration when available.
Do not put token values in this file.

servers.name: example-database
notes: Use only when a task requires real database access.
Prefer read-only access for analysis tasks.

missing_mcp_for_task.instruction: Tell the human which MCP server is required, why it is required, and ask Env Configr to configure it.

mcp_not_needed_yet.instruction: Record as deferred. Do not require MCP setup during early project setup if no current task needs it.

credentials_missing.instruction: Ask the human to provide credentials through the secure mechanism supported by the selected platform.
